metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_153
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Using immuno electron microscopy, it is shown that PKM-zeta (a splice variant of the PRKCZ gene) is present at the PSD. No staining was observed in the presynaptic terminal. The authors claim that the staining is no present in KO controls, but do not provide the data for this.

13/3/2017 Pim:
- Used antibody does not differentiate between PKCzeta and PKMzeta (isoform of PKCzeta gene). Authors state: "PKMζ mRNA and protein are expressed almost exclusively in brain, whereas the full-length PKCζ mRNA and protein are not expressed in forebrain, except in the lateral olfactory tract [3,5,6]. Therefore, in hippocampus and neocortex, immunostaining with antisera to ζ will specifically determine the localization of PKMζ.". Experimental description: Male two to three month-old Sprague-Dawley rats were anaes-
thetized by intraperitoneal injection with sodium pentobarbital
(45 mg/60 g body weight, Anpro Pharmaceutical, Arcadia, CA,
USA) before intracardiac perfusion, according to State University
of New York Downstate Medical Center Animal Use and Care
Committee standards.
